failedAssertions.reason: FALSE POSITIVE: npm run release-check exits with code 0 but secret-scan step reports false positives from .factory/validation/ directory. Round 2 user testing confirmed all 9 personal absolute path matches are in .factory/validation/ subdirectories (documentation/, governance/, security/) and represent validation report metadata for test isolation context, not actual source code. Source code verification excluding .factory/validation/ showed the actual codebase is clean with no PII or secrets. The secret-scan.sh script does not exclude .factory/validation/ directory, causing false positives on validation artifacts that are intentionally committed per CLAUDE.md. Fix: Update scripts/secret-scan.sh line 32 to add ':(exclude).factory/validation' to git grep command.

appliedUpdates.description: Flow validator confirmed repository-state validation guidance is accurate. No updates needed - isolation rules, testing tools, and assertion guidance are comprehensive.

discoveredIssues.description: secret-scan.sh script does not exclude .factory/validation/ directory, causing false positives on validation report artifacts. The validation reports contain personal absolute paths (basePath, workingDirectory, evidenceDirectory fields) as part of their normal operation, but these are infrastructure artifacts, not source code.
discoveredIssues.suggestedFix: Update scripts/secret-scan.sh line 32 to add ':(exclude).factory/validation' to the git grep command for user-specific absolute path detection. This allows validation reports to be committed without triggering false positives while still catching actual PII in source code.
